title: Seasoned Mixtures and Salts
content:
SEASONED MIXTURES

Chili Powder: Based on cumin, coriander, oregano, peppers. Simmer food at least 15 minutes after adding to heighten flavor.

Five-Spices Powder: Equal amounts Chinese star anise, fennel, pepper, cloves, cinnamon. For red-stews, roasted meats.

Four Spices (Quatre Épices): 1 teaspoon each cloves, nutmeg, ginger + 1 tablespoon cinnamon. For sweets and meats.

Cinnamon Sugar: 1 cup sugar + 2 tablespoons cinnamon.

Citrus Sugars: 1-2 tablespoons grated citrus rind per cup sugar. Store covered cool.

Vanilla Sugar: 1-2 vanilla beans in 2 cups sugar; or crush beans with few tablespoons sugar, add to storage sugar.

SALTS

General: Enhances flavors of other foods; preserves by drawing moisture and creating brine. Add judiciously toward end of cooking except where needed early for clarification or yeast control.

Types:
- Table Salt: Finely ground, 90% sodium chloride, free-flowing
- Iodized: Recommended where soil/water lack iodine
- Coarse/Kosher/Sea: Squarish grains, natural minerals; flavorful for cooking and finishing
- Pickling/Dairy: Pure, no additives; won't cloud liquids
- Rock Salt: Non-edible; used for freezing ice cream, baking potatoes, heating oysters

Brine: 10% solution = 1½ cups salt per gallon (or 6 tablespoons per quart) = strong enough to float 2-ounce egg with shell just breaking surface.

Removing Excess Salt: Parblanch bacon or salt pork in cold water brought slowly to boil; simmer 3-10 minutes depending on dice size.

Anchovies: ¼ anchovy or ⅛ teaspoon paste per cup sauce adds piquancy. Soak in cold water or milk ½-1 hour for salads; 5-10 minutes warm water for sauces.

Monosodium Glutamate (MSG): Intensifies some flavors; use sparingly if at all. Soluble in water, not fat. Some people allergic ("Chinese Restaurant Syndrome").
